'''Robert Thomas Roberts''' (18 February 1912 – September 1979) was an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1930s and 1940s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], as a {{rlp|ce|pr|hk|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

==Background==
Bob Roberts' birth was registered in  [[Prescot]] district, [[Lancashire]], England, he was living in Gartons Lane, [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] and working as a [[clerk]] at a [[Truck scale|weighbridge]] {{circa|1939}}, and he died aged 67 in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], [[Merseyside]], England.

==Playing career==

===International honours===
Roberts won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Widnes in 1939 against Wales, and in 1940 against Wales.

===Championship final appearances===
Roberts played in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 2-21 defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]] Final during the [[1935–36 Northern Rugby Football League season#Championship play-offs|1935–36 season]].

===Challenge Cup Final appearances===
Roberts played in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 18-5 victory over [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] in the [[1936–37 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1936–37 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1936–37 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 8 May 1937.

===County Cup Final appearances===
Roberts played {{rlp|lf}} in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 4-5 defeat by [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[1939–40 Lancashire Cup]] Final first-leg during the [[1939–40 Northern Rugby Football League Wartime Emergency League season|1939–40 season]] at [[Halton Stadium|Naughton Park]], [[Widnes]] on Saturday 20 April 1940, and played at {{rlp|sr}} in the 11-16 defeat (15-21 aggregate defeat) by [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in the [[1939–40 Lancashire Cup]] Final second-leg during the [[1939–40 Northern Rugby Football League Wartime Emergency League season|1939–40 season]] at [[Station Road, Swinton]] on Saturday 27 April 1940, and played at {{rlp|sr}} in [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]' 7-3 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1945–46 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1945–46 Northern Rugby Football League season|1945–46 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]] on Saturday 27 October 1945.

===Club career===
Roberts made his début for Widnes as a {{rlp|ce}} against [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] at [[Craven Park, Barrow-in-Furness]] on Monday 17 April 1933, and he played his last match for Widnes against [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] at [[Odsal Stadium]], [[Bradford]] on Saturday 27 September 1947, he left Widnes for St. Helens for £800/£1000 (based on increases in average earnings, this would be approximately £71,110/£88,887 in 2013),<ref name="Measuring Worth - Relative Value of UK Pounds">{{cite web|url=http://www.measuringworth.com/ukcompare|title=Measuring Worth - Relative Value of UK Pounds|publisher=Measuring Worth|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015}}</ref>
and he made his début for St. Helens in the 24-3 victory over [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Saturday 4 October 1947, and he played his last match for St. Helens in the 10-12 defeat by [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] at [[Cougar Park|Lawkholme Lane]], [[Keighley]] on Saturday 23 October 1948.
